    I'M NEW

    Living Savior's Mission is to know the love of Christ and serve God so that the Holy Spirit may work through us to bring others to Christ.

    Preschool

    We believe that Living Savior Preschool can expand the child's perception of God, of other people, and of the physical world in which God has placed us.

  • Calendar
  • Contact
  2. Ministries
Ministries Listed Alphabetically

Communication | David Huxsoll

The communication ministry is responsible for “getting the word out”, about Living Savior Lutheran Church activities to both the congregation and the community. Provides oversight of multiple forms of communication to include letters, press engagement, website, newsletter, church TV monitors, and Facebook page, as well as broadcast email and text systems. Other individuals that support communication ministry:

  • Cameron Gallarno – webmaster.
  • Mary Heetland - Newsletter editor.
  • Lori Hults - LSLC Facebook.
  • Church Secretary - Broadcast emails, TV Monitor, and calendar updates.

Confirmation | Pastor Andy Lissy

Our confirmation classes teach the basic building blocks of the Lutheran faith, so that our young people can apply these truths to their lives.  Confirmation instruction is only one stage in a life-long growing process in our Christian faith. Attending confirmation classes provides our youth an opportunity to grow in their faith and set a course to develop their understanding of Christ's teachings.

Education | Shari Hernandez

The Education ministry serves Living Savior Lutheran Church by providing congregation members of all ages with the appropriate programs that foster Christian spiritual growth, Bible exploration, connections to Martin Luther's Small Catechism, and an understanding of Lutheran Church traditions. Children, young adult, and adult Sunday School classes and summer Vacation Bible School are provided to promote Christian Education. Bring your entire family and join us on Sunday mornings and feed your soul!

Elders | Jim Neuburger

We are grateful servants of God, servants of scripture, and servants of the worship space.  Greeters welcome members and visitors as they arrive. Ushers assist with worship material distribution, seating, and offering collection. Lay Readers read Scripture that becomes the basis for our personal understanding of God’s love. The Altar Guild faithfully cares for and prepares our sacramental vessels, fair linen, paraments, flowers and, most importantly, our Lord’s Supper.  May the power of the Holy Spirit inspire you to serve in the Worship Ministry and provide to you an uplifting and rewarding experience.

Fellowship | Jenny Minor

The Fellowship Ministry celebrates Christian Fellowship throughout the year by providing a variety of Christ-centered activities and events. Some of our previous Fellowship events are Mystery Dinner, Christmas Progressive Dinner, Suppers of Eight, Family Bingo Night, Chili Cook-off, and Wine Tasting. Events this year will include a Cooking Demonstration by Chef Chaz, a 1950s themed Valentine Dinner, Family BBQ & Country Concert, and Rally Day Picnic. Come join the fun!

Music | Lois Finnern, Interim - Dr. Wally Horton

The Music Ministry offers many opportunities for personal involvement in the Worship Service. The Chancel Choir leads the singing of hymns and liturgy, presents a scripture-based anthem each Sunday, and performs an annual Christmas Cantata. Instrumentalists and vocalists in the Contemporary Praise Band lead the Worship Service with modern songs. Solos and musical ensembles provide opportunities for worshipers to participate. Sunday school children perform a Christmas Program. Music has the power to light up scripture, give life to the words, impress it on the heart, and express the joy it brings!

Outreach | Ric Weyrick

In truth, every Ministry (and every congregant!) at Living Savior is a means to Evangelize.  We reach out to the community to share the Gospel by both word and deed.  Sometimes in partnership with other Ministries such as Fellowship, Social, and Music, we host and participate in community outreach efforts such as concerts, Open Houses, picnics, Christmas Tree sales, and other service events to which we invite friends, neighbors, and co-workers, while advertising them as well to include our local community.  We are also heavily involved with our Vicar in the expanding George Mason University Campus Ministry.  Make a difference, and become an active part of fulfilling our Great Commission - join us in helping spread the Good News!

Preschool | Interim - Liz Smith

At Living Savior Preschool, our program offers young children one of the best opportunities for achieving the goals of a Christian education outside of the home.  Our school can help children develop healthy personalities, form values and develop the conscience, feelings, and attitudes which build the foundation for a positive self-image and good relationships.

Property | Marc Morgan

Living Savior is blessed with a beautiful sanctuary and a thriving preschool. The purchase of the North property added the Vicar’s house and several more acres of land. Between maintenance of the buildings and the extensive property there is always a need. If you have skills in carpentry, painting, electrical, plumbing, a desire to crank up your chain saw, or just a willingness to devote your time and labor there is a place for you with the Property Ministry! We support large work days in the Spring and Fall, and there are always on-going projects throughout the year.

Social | Miki Kolton and Steve Thomas

At Living Savior, we rejoice in knowing that we are the Body of Christ.  We are called to love one another and to feed His sheep.  Join us as we serve Him by serving others as the “hands and feet” of our Savior by participating in the Social Ministry projects: Crop Walk, Shelter House/NOVACO (Back to School supplies, Thanksgiving boxes, Christmas Angel Tree, tutoring, translation services, meals, emergency supplies etc.), Fellowship Square Foundation pantry items, Concordia Food Ministry, Operation Christmas Child, meal packing for Central Union Mission, meals at hypothermia shelter, Wounded Warrior Chaplain’s Closet, disaster relief, refugee support and more.

Stewardship | Larry and Terri Beam

Living Savior’s Stewardship Ministry focuses on Christian stewardship, which is the free and joyous activity of the child of God, and God’s family, the church, in managing all of life and life’s resources, for God’s purpose. In Christ we are freed to give and freed to serve!

Young Family

Young family ministry is a fellowship and faith enriching opportunity for the family and friends of Living Savior. We meet once a month for an activity to have some fun, build relationships, and experience God’s word in action. Our primary focus is families with children from preschool age through fifth grade, but we welcome all families! We seek to create an environment where the children and families of Living Savior know and feel their importance to the Church body.

Youth | Jonathan and Sarah Loesch

The Youth Ministry provides an opportunity for youth, grades 6 through 12, to socialize, try new experiences, and discuss things that are important in their lives.  We meet twice a month and have dinner, play games, and have a short discussion.  We also take trips to places such as Rebounderz, canoeing, rock climbing, and bowling.

at 10:00 until 10:45am

For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life. (John 3:16)
